NKJV.1: A Contemplation of Asaph. Give ear, O my people, to my law; Incline your ears to the words of my mouth.
NKJV.2: I will open my mouth in a parable; I will utter dark sayings of old,
NKJV.3: Which we have heard and known, And our fathers have told us.
NKJV.4: We will not hide them from their children, Telling to the generation to come the praises of the LORD, And His strength and His wonderful works that He has done.
NKJV.5: For He established a testimony in Jacob, And appointed a law in Israel, Which He commanded our fathers, That they should make them known to their children;
NKJV.6: That the generation to come might know them, The children who would be born, That they may arise and declare them to their children,
NKJV.7: That they may set their hope in God, And not forget the works of God, But keep His commandments;
NKJV.8: And may not be like their fathers, A stubborn and rebellious generation, A generation that did not set its heart aright, And whose spirit was not faithful to God.
NKJV.9: The children of Ephraim, being armed and carrying bows, Turned back in the day of battle.
NKJV.10: They did not keep the covenant of God; They refused to walk in His law,
NKJV.11: And forgot His works And His wonders that He had shown them.
NKJV.12: Marvelous things He did in the sight of their fathers, In the land of Egypt, in the field of Zoan.
NKJV.13: He divided the sea and caused them to pass through; And He made the waters stand up like a heap.
NKJV.14: In the daytime also He led them with the cloud, And all the night with a light of fire.
NKJV.15: He split the rocks in the wilderness, And gave them drink in abundance like the depths.
NKJV.16: He also brought streams out of the rock, And caused waters to run down like rivers.
NKJV.17: But they sinned even more against Him By rebelling against the Most High in the wilderness.
NKJV.18: And they tested God in their heart By asking for the food of their fancy.
NKJV.19: Yes, they spoke against God: They said, “Can God prepare a table in the wilderness?
NKJV.20: Behold, He struck the rock, So that the waters gushed out, And the streams overflowed. Can He give bread also? Can He provide meat for His people?”
NKJV.21: Therefore the LORD heard this and was furious; So a fire was kindled against Jacob, And anger also came up against Israel,
NKJV.22: Because they did not believe in God, And did not trust in His salvation.
NKJV.23: Yet He had commanded the clouds above,  And opened the doors of heaven,
NKJV.24: Had rained down manna on them to eat, And given them of the bread of heaven.
NKJV.25: Men ate angels’ food; He sent them food to the full.
NKJV.26: He caused an east wind to blow in the heavens; And by His power He brought in the south wind.
NKJV.27: He also rained meat on them like the dust, Feathered fowl like the sand of the seas;
NKJV.28: And He let them fall in the midst of their camp, All around their dwellings.
NKJV.29: So they ate and were well filled, For He gave them their own desire.
NKJV.30: They were not deprived of their craving; But while their food was still in their mouths,
NKJV.31: The wrath of God came against them, And slew the stoutest of them, And struck down the choice men of Israel.
NKJV.32: In spite of this they still sinned, And did not believe in His wondrous works.
NKJV.33: Therefore their days He consumed in futility, And their years in fear.
NKJV.34: When He slew them, then they sought Him; And they returned and sought earnestly for God.
NKJV.35: Then they remembered that God was their rock, And the Most High God their Redeemer.
NKJV.36: Nevertheless they flattered Him with their mouth, And they lied to Him with their tongue;
NKJV.37: For their heart was not steadfast with Him, Nor were they faithful in His covenant.
NKJV.38: But He, being full of compassion, forgave their iniquity, And did not destroy them. Yes, many a time He turned His anger away, And did not stir up all His wrath;
NKJV.39: For He remembered that they were but flesh, A breath that passes away and does not come again.
NKJV.40: How often they provoked Him in the wilderness, And grieved Him in the desert!
NKJV.41: Yes, again and again they tempted God, And limited the Holy One of Israel.
NKJV.42: They did not remember His power: The day when He redeemed them from the enemy,
NKJV.43: When He worked His signs in Egypt, And His wonders in the field of Zoan;
NKJV.44: Turned their rivers into blood, And their streams, that they could not drink.
NKJV.45: He sent swarms of flies among them, which devoured them, And frogs, which destroyed them.
NKJV.46: He also gave their crops to the caterpillar, And their labor to the locust.
NKJV.47: He destroyed their vines with hail, And their sycamore trees with frost.
NKJV.48: He also gave up their cattle to the hail, And their flocks to fiery lightning.
NKJV.49: He cast on them the fierceness of His anger, Wrath, indignation, and trouble, By sending angels of destruction among them.
NKJV.50: He made a path for His anger; He did not spare their soul from death, But gave their life over to the plague,
NKJV.51: And destroyed all the firstborn in Egypt, The first of their strength in the tents of Ham.
NKJV.52: But He made His own people go forth like sheep, And guided them in the wilderness like a flock;
NKJV.53: And He led them on safely, so that they did not fear; But the sea overwhelmed their enemies.
NKJV.54: And He brought them to His holy border, This mountain which His right hand had acquired.
NKJV.55: He also drove out the nations before them,  Allotted them an inheritance by survey, And made the tribes of Israel dwell in their tents.
NKJV.56: Yet they tested and provoked the Most High God, And did not keep His testimonies,
NKJV.57: But turned back and acted unfaithfully like their fathers; They were turned aside like a deceitful bow.
NKJV.58: For they provoked Him to anger with their high places, And moved Him to jealousy with their carved images.
NKJV.59: When God heard this, He was furious, And greatly abhorred Israel,
NKJV.60: So that He forsook the tabernacle of Shiloh, The tent He had placed among men,
NKJV.61: And delivered His strength into captivity, And His glory into the enemy’s hand.
NKJV.62: He also gave His people over to the sword, And was furious with His inheritance.
NKJV.63: The fire consumed their young men, And their maidens were not given in marriage.
NKJV.64: Their priests fell by the sword, And their widows made no lamentation.
NKJV.65: Then the Lord awoke as from sleep, Like a mighty man who shouts because of wine.
NKJV.66: And He beat back His enemies; He put them to a perpetual reproach.
NKJV.67: Moreover He rejected the tent of Joseph, And did not choose the tribe of Ephraim,
NKJV.68: But chose the tribe of Judah, Mount Zion which He loved.
NKJV.69: And He built His sanctuary like the heights, Like the earth which He has established forever.
NKJV.70: He also chose David His servant, And took him from the sheepfolds;
NKJV.71: From following the ewes that had young He brought him,  To shepherd Jacob His people, And Israel His inheritance.
NKJV.72: So he shepherded them according to the integrity of his heart, And guided them by the skillfulness of his hands.

ESV.1: Give ear, O my people, to my teaching; incline your ears to the words of my mouth!
ESV.2: I will open my mouth in a parable; I will utter dark sayings from of old,
ESV.3: things that we have heard and known, that our fathers have told us.
ESV.4: We will not hide them from their children, but tell to the coming generation the glorious deeds of the LORD, and his might, and the wonders that he has done.
ESV.5: He established a testimony in Jacob and appointed a law in Israel, which he commanded our fathers to teach to their children,
ESV.6: that the next generation might know them, the children yet unborn, and arise and tell them to their children,
ESV.7: so that they should set their hope in God and not forget the works of God, but keep his commandments;
ESV.8: and that they should not be like their fathers, a stubborn and rebellious generation, a generation whose heart was not steadfast, whose spirit was not faithful to God.
ESV.9: The Ephraimites, armed with  the bow, turned back on the day of battle.
ESV.10: They did not keep God's covenant, but refused to walk according to his law.
ESV.11: They forgot his works and the wonders that he had shown them.
ESV.12: In the sight of their fathers he performed wonders in the land of Egypt, in the fields of Zoan.
ESV.13: He divided the sea and let them pass through it, and made the waters stand like a heap.
ESV.14: In the daytime he led them with a cloud, and all the night with a fiery light.
ESV.15: He split rocks in the wilderness and gave them drink abundantly as from the deep.
ESV.16: He made streams come out of the rock and caused waters to flow down like rivers.
ESV.17: Yet they sinned still more against him, rebelling against the Most High in the desert.
ESV.18: They tested God in their heart by demanding the food they craved.
ESV.19: They spoke against God, saying, “Can God spread a table in the wilderness?
ESV.20: He struck the rock so that water gushed out and streams overflowed. Can he also give bread or provide meat for his people?”
ESV.21: Therefore, when the LORD heard, he was full of wrath; a fire was kindled against Jacob; his anger rose against Israel,
ESV.22: because they did not believe in God and did not trust his saving power.
ESV.23: Yet he commanded the skies above and opened the doors of heaven,
ESV.24: and he rained down on them manna to eat and gave them the grain of heaven.
ESV.25: Man ate of the bread of the angels; he sent them food in abundance.
ESV.26: He caused the east wind to blow in the heavens, and by his power he led out the south wind;
ESV.27: he rained meat on them like dust, winged birds like the sand of the seas;
ESV.28: he let them fall in the midst of their camp, all around their dwellings.
ESV.29: And they ate and were well filled, for he gave them what they craved.
ESV.30: But before they had satisfied their craving, while the food was still in their mouths,
ESV.31: the anger of God rose against them, and he killed the strongest of them and laid low the young men of Israel.
ESV.32: In spite of all this, they still sinned; despite his wonders, they did not believe.
ESV.33: So he made their days vanish like  a breath,  and their years in terror.
ESV.34: When he killed them, they sought him; they repented and sought God earnestly.
ESV.35: They remembered that God was their rock, the Most High God their redeemer.
ESV.36: But they flattered him with their mouths; they lied to him with their tongues.
ESV.37: Their heart was not steadfast toward him; they were not faithful to his covenant.
ESV.38: Yet he, being compassionate, atoned for their iniquity and did not destroy them; he restrained his anger often and did not stir up all his wrath.
ESV.39: He remembered that they were but flesh, a wind that passes and comes not again.
ESV.40: How often they rebelled against him in the wilderness and grieved him in the desert!
ESV.41: They tested God again and again and provoked the Holy One of Israel.
ESV.42: They did not remember his power  or the day when he redeemed them from the foe,
ESV.43: when he performed his signs in Egypt and his marvels in the fields of Zoan.
ESV.44: He turned their rivers to blood, so that they could not drink of their streams.
ESV.45: He sent among them swarms of flies, which devoured them, and frogs, which destroyed them.
ESV.46: He gave their crops to the destroying locust and the fruit of their labor to the locust.
ESV.47: He destroyed their vines with hail and their sycamores with frost.
ESV.48: He gave over their cattle to the hail and their flocks to thunderbolts.
ESV.49: He let loose on them his burning anger, wrath, indignation, and distress, a company of destroying angels.
ESV.50: He made a path for his anger; he did not spare them from death, but gave their lives over to the plague.
ESV.51: He struck down every firstborn in Egypt, the firstfruits of their strength in the tents of Ham.
ESV.52: Then he led out his people like sheep and guided them in the wilderness like a flock.
ESV.53: He led them in safety, so that they were not afraid, but the sea overwhelmed their enemies.
ESV.54: And he brought them to his holy land, to the mountain which his right hand had won.
ESV.55: He drove out nations before them; he apportioned them for a possession and settled the tribes of Israel in their tents.
ESV.56: Yet they tested and rebelled against the Most High God and did not keep his testimonies,
ESV.57: but turned away and acted treacherously like their fathers; they twisted like a deceitful bow.
ESV.58: For they provoked him to anger with their high places; they moved him to jealousy with their idols.
ESV.59: When God heard, he was full of wrath, and he utterly rejected Israel.
ESV.60: He forsook his dwelling at Shiloh, the tent where he dwelt among mankind,
ESV.61: and delivered his power to captivity, his glory to the hand of the foe.
ESV.62: He gave his people over to the sword and vented his wrath on his heritage.
ESV.63: Fire devoured their young men, and their young women had no marriage song.
ESV.64: Their priests fell by the sword, and their widows made no lamentation.
ESV.65: Then the Lord awoke as from sleep, like a strong man shouting because of wine.
ESV.66: And he put his adversaries to rout; he put them to everlasting shame.
ESV.67: He rejected the tent of Joseph; he did not choose the tribe of Ephraim,
ESV.68: but he chose the tribe of Judah, Mount Zion, which he loves.
ESV.69: He built his sanctuary like the high heavens, like the earth, which he has founded forever.
ESV.70: He chose David his servant and took him from the sheepfolds;
ESV.71: from following the nursing ewes he brought him to shepherd Jacob his people, Israel his inheritance.
ESV.72: With upright heart he shepherded them and guided them with his skillful hand.

NASB.1: A Maskil of Asaph. Listen, O my people, to my instruction; Incline your ears to the words of my mouth.
NASB.2: I will open my mouth in a parable; I will utter dark sayings of old,
NASB.3: Which we have heard and known, And our fathers have told us.
NASB.4: We will not conceal them from their children, But tell to the generation to come the praises of the LORD, And His strength and His wondrous works that He has done.
NASB.5: For He established a testimony in Jacob And appointed a law in Israel, Which He commanded our fathers That they should teach them to their children,
NASB.6: That the generation to come might know, [even] the children [yet] to be born, [That] they may arise and tell [them] to their children,
NASB.7: That they should put their confidence in God And not forget the works of God, But keep His commandments,
NASB.8: And not be like their fathers, A stubborn and rebellious generation, A generation that did not prepare its heart And whose spirit was not faithful to God.
NASB.9: The sons of Ephraim were archers equipped with bows, [Yet] they turned back in the day of battle.
NASB.10: They did not keep the covenant of God And refused to walk in His law;
NASB.11: They forgot His deeds And His miracles that He had shown them.
NASB.12: He wrought wonders before their fathers In the land of Egypt, in the field of Zoan.
NASB.13: He divided the sea and caused them to pass through, And He made the waters stand up like a heap.
NASB.14: Then He led them with the cloud by day And all the night with a light of fire.
NASB.15: He split the rocks in the wilderness And gave [them] abundant drink like the ocean depths.
NASB.16: He brought forth streams also from the rock And caused waters to run down like rivers.
NASB.17: Yet they still continued to sin against Him, To rebel against the Most High in the desert.
NASB.18: And in their heart they put God to the test By asking food according to their desire.
NASB.19: Then they spoke against God; They said, 'Can God prepare a table in the wilderness?
NASB.20: 'Behold, He struck the rock so that waters gushed out, And streams were overflowing; Can He give bread also? Will He provide meat for His people?'
NASB.21: Therefore the LORD heard and was full of wrath; And a fire was kindled against Jacob And anger also mounted against Israel,
NASB.22: Because they did not believe in God And did not trust in His salvation.
NASB.23: Yet He commanded the clouds above And opened the doors of heaven;
NASB.24: He rained down manna upon them to eat And gave them food from heaven.
NASB.25: Man did eat the bread of angels; He sent them food in abundance.
NASB.26: He caused the east wind to blow in the heavens And by His power He directed the south wind.
NASB.27: When He rained meat upon them like the dust, Even winged fowl like the sand of the seas,
NASB.28: Then He let [them] fall in the midst of their camp, Round about their dwellings.
NASB.29: So they ate and were well filled, And their desire He gave to them.
NASB.30: Before they had satisfied their desire, While their food was in their mouths,
NASB.31: The anger of God rose against them And killed some of their stoutest ones, And subdued the choice men of Israel.
NASB.32: In spite of all this they still sinned And did not believe in His wonderful works.
NASB.33: So He brought their days to an end in futility And their years in sudden terror.
NASB.34: When He killed them, then they sought Him, And returned and searched diligently for God;
NASB.35: And they remembered that God was their rock, And the Most High God their Redeemer.
NASB.36: But they deceived Him with their mouth And lied to Him with their tongue.
NASB.37: For their heart was not steadfast toward Him, Nor were they faithful in His covenant.
NASB.38: But He, being compassionate, forgave [their] iniquity and did not destroy [them]; And often He restrained His anger And did not arouse all His wrath.
NASB.39: Thus He remembered that they were but flesh, A wind that passes and does not return.
NASB.40: How often they rebelled against Him in the wilderness And grieved Him in the desert!
NASB.41: Again and again they tempted God, And pained the Holy One of Israel.
NASB.42: They did not remember His power, The day when He redeemed them from the adversary,
NASB.43: When He performed His signs in Egypt And His marvels in the field of Zoan,
NASB.44: And turned their rivers to blood, And their streams, they could not drink.
NASB.45: He sent among them swarms of flies which devoured them, And frogs which destroyed them.
NASB.46: He gave also their crops to the grasshopper And the product of their labor to the locust.
NASB.47: He destroyed their vines with hailstones And their sycamore trees with frost.
NASB.48: He gave over their cattle also to the hailstones And their herds to bolts of lightning.
NASB.49: He sent upon them His burning anger, Fury and indignation and trouble, A band of destroying angels.
NASB.50: He leveled a path for His anger; He did not spare their soul from death, But gave over their life to the plague,
NASB.51: And smote all the firstborn in Egypt, The first [issue] of their virility in the tents of Ham.
NASB.52: But He led forth His own people like sheep And guided them in the wilderness like a flock;
NASB.53: He led them safely, so that they did not fear; But the sea engulfed their enemies.
NASB.54: So He brought them to His holy land, To this hill country which His right hand had gained.
NASB.55: He also drove out the nations before them And apportioned them for an inheritance by measurement, And made the tribes of Israel dwell in their tents.
NASB.56: Yet they tempted and rebelled against the Most High God And did not keep His testimonies,
NASB.57: But turned back and acted treacherously like their fathers; They turned aside like a treacherous bow.
NASB.58: For they provoked Him with their high places And aroused His jealousy with their graven images.
NASB.59: When God heard, He was filled with wrath And greatly abhorred Israel;
NASB.60: So that He abandoned the dwelling place at Shiloh, The tent which He had pitched among men,
NASB.61: And gave up His strength to captivity And His glory into the hand of the adversary.
NASB.62: He also delivered His people to the sword, And was filled with wrath at His inheritance.
NASB.63: Fire devoured His young men, And His virgins had no wedding songs.
NASB.64: His priests fell by the sword, And His widows could not weep.
NASB.65: Then the Lord awoke as [if from] sleep, Like a warrior overcome by wine.
NASB.66: He drove His adversaries backward; He put on them an everlasting reproach.
NASB.67: He also rejected the tent of Joseph, And did not choose the tribe of Ephraim,
NASB.68: But chose the tribe of Judah, Mount Zion which He loved.
NASB.69: And He built His sanctuary like the heights, Like the earth which He has founded forever.
NASB.70: He also chose David His servant And took him from the sheepfolds;
NASB.71: From the care of the ewes with suckling lambs He brought him To shepherd Jacob His people, And Israel His inheritance.
NASB.72: So he shepherded them according to the integrity of his heart, And guided them with his skillful hands.

NLT.1:
O my people, listen to my instructions.
Open your ears to what I am saying,
NLT.2:
for I will speak to you in a parable.
I will teach you hidden lessons from our past —
NLT.3:
stories we have heard and known,
stories our ancestors handed down to us.
NLT.4:
We will not hide these truths from our children;
we will tell the next generation
about the glorious deeds of the LORD,
about his power and his mighty wonders.
NLT.5:
For he issued his laws to Jacob;
he gave his instructions to Israel.
He commanded our ancestors
to teach them to their children,
NLT.6:
so the next generation might know them —
even the children not yet born —
and they in turn will teach their own children.
NLT.7:
So each generation should set its hope anew on God,
not forgetting his glorious miracles
and obeying his commands.
NLT.8:
Then they will not be like their ancestors —
stubborn, rebellious, and unfaithful,
refusing to give their hearts to God.
NLT.9:
The warriors of Ephraim, though armed with bows,
turned their backs and fled on the day of battle.
NLT.10:
They did not keep God’s covenant
and refused to live by his instructions.
NLT.11:
They forgot what he had done —
the great wonders he had shown them,
NLT.12:
the miracles he did for their ancestors
on the plain of Zoan in the land of Egypt.
NLT.13:
For he divided the sea and led them through,
making the water stand up like walls!
NLT.14:
In the daytime he led them by a cloud,
and all night by a pillar of fire.
NLT.15:
He split open the rocks in the wilderness
to give them water, as from a gushing spring.
NLT.16:
He made streams pour from the rock,
making the waters flow down like a river!
NLT.17:
Yet they kept on sinning against him,
rebelling against the Most High in the desert.
NLT.18:
They stubbornly tested God in their hearts,
demanding the foods they craved.
NLT.19:
They even spoke against God himself, saying,
“God can’t give us food in the wilderness.
NLT.20:
Yes, he can strike a rock so water gushes out,
but he can’t give his people bread and meat.”
NLT.21:
When the LORD heard them, he was furious.
The fire of his wrath burned against Jacob.
Yes, his anger rose against Israel,
NLT.22:
for they did not believe God
or trust him to care for them.
NLT.23:
But he commanded the skies to open;
he opened the doors of heaven.
NLT.24:
He rained down manna for them to eat;
he gave them bread from heaven.
NLT.25:
They ate the food of angels!
God gave them all they could hold.
NLT.26:
He released the east wind in the heavens
and guided the south wind by his mighty power.
NLT.27:
He rained down meat as thick as dust —
birds as plentiful as the sand on the seashore!
NLT.28:
He caused the birds to fall within their camp
and all around their tents.
NLT.29:
The people ate their fill.
He gave them what they craved.
NLT.30:
But before they satisfied their craving,
while the meat was yet in their mouths,
NLT.31:
the anger of God rose against them,
and he killed their strongest men.
He struck down the finest of Israel’s young men.
NLT.32:
But in spite of this, the people kept sinning.
Despite his wonders, they refused to trust him.
NLT.33:
So he ended their lives in failure,
their years in terror.
NLT.34:
When God began killing them,
they finally sought him.
They repented and took God seriously.
NLT.35:
Then they remembered that God was their rock,
that God Most High was their redeemer.
NLT.36:
But all they gave him was lip service;
they lied to him with their tongues.
NLT.37:
Their hearts were not loyal to him.
They did not keep his covenant.
NLT.38:
Yet he was merciful and forgave their sins
and did not destroy them all.
Many times he held back his anger
and did not unleash his fury!
NLT.39:
For he remembered that they were merely mortal,
gone like a breath of wind that never returns.
NLT.40:
Oh, how often they rebelled against him in the wilderness
and grieved his heart in that dry wasteland.
NLT.41:
Again and again they tested God’s patience
and provoked the Holy One of Israel.
NLT.42:
They did not remember his power
and how he rescued them from their enemies.
NLT.43:
They did not remember his miraculous signs in Egypt,
his wonders on the plain of Zoan.
NLT.44:
For he turned their rivers into blood,
so no one could drink from the streams.
NLT.45:
He sent vast swarms of flies to consume them
and hordes of frogs to ruin them.
NLT.46:
He gave their crops to caterpillars;
their harvest was consumed by locusts.
NLT.47:
He destroyed their grapevines with hail
and shattered their sycamore-figs with sleet.
NLT.48:
He abandoned their cattle to the hail,
their livestock to bolts of lightning.
NLT.49:
He loosed on them his fierce anger —
all his fury, rage, and hostility.
He dispatched against them
a band of destroying angels.
NLT.50:
He turned his anger against them;
he did not spare the Egyptians’ lives
but ravaged them with the plague.
NLT.51:
He killed the oldest son in each Egyptian family,
the flower of youth throughout the land of Egypt.
NLT.52:
But he led his own people like a flock of sheep,
guiding them safely through the wilderness.
NLT.53:
He kept them safe so they were not afraid;
but the sea covered their enemies.
NLT.54:
He brought them to the border of his holy land,
to this land of hills he had won for them.
NLT.55:
He drove out the nations before them;
he gave them their inheritance by lot.
He settled the tribes of Israel into their homes.
NLT.56:
But they kept testing and rebelling against God Most High.
They did not obey his laws.
NLT.57:
They turned back and were as faithless as their parents.
They were as undependable as a crooked bow.
NLT.58:
They angered God by building shrines to other gods;
they made him jealous with their idols.
NLT.59:
When God heard them, he was very angry,
and he completely rejected Israel.
NLT.60:
Then he abandoned his dwelling at Shiloh,
the Tabernacle where he had lived among the people.
NLT.61:
He allowed the Ark of his might to be captured;
he surrendered his glory into enemy hands.
NLT.62:
He gave his people over to be butchered by the sword,
because he was so angry with his own people — his special possession.
NLT.63:
Their young men were killed by fire;
their young women died before singing their wedding songs.
NLT.64:
Their priests were slaughtered,
and their widows could not mourn their deaths.
NLT.65:
Then the Lord rose up as though waking from sleep,
like a warrior aroused from a drunken stupor.
NLT.66:
He routed his enemies
and sent them to eternal shame.
NLT.67:
But he rejected Joseph’s descendants;
he did not choose the tribe of Ephraim.
NLT.68:
He chose instead the tribe of Judah,
and Mount Zion, which he loved.
NLT.69:
There he built his sanctuary as high as the heavens,
as solid and enduring as the earth.
NLT.70:
He chose his servant David,
calling him from the sheep pens.
NLT.71:
He took David from tending the ewes and lambs
and made him the shepherd of Jacob’s descendants —
God’s own people, Israel.
NLT.72:
He cared for them with a true heart
and led them with skillful hands.

WEB.1: A contemplation by Asaph. Hear my teaching, my people. Turn your ears to the words of my mouth.
WEB.2: I will open my mouth in a parable. I will utter dark sayings of old,
WEB.3: Which we have heard and known, and our fathers have told us.
WEB.4: We will not hide them from their children, telling to the generation to come the praises of Yahweh, his strength, and his wondrous deeds that he has done.
WEB.5: For he established a testimony in Jacob, and appointed a teaching in Israel, which he commanded our fathers, that they should make them known to their children;
WEB.6: that the generation to come might know, even the children who should be born; who should arise and tell their children,
WEB.7: that they might set their hope in God, and not forget God’s deeds, but keep his commandments,
WEB.8: and might not be as their fathers, a stubborn and rebellious generation, a generation that didn’t make their hearts loyal, whose spirit was not steadfast with God.
WEB.9: The children of Ephraim, being armed and carrying bows, turned back in the day of battle.
WEB.10: They didn’t keep God’s covenant, and refused to walk in his law.
WEB.11: They forgot his doings, his wondrous deeds that he had shown them.
WEB.12: He did marvelous things in the sight of their fathers, in the land of Egypt, in the field of Zoan.
WEB.13: He split the sea, and caused them to pass through. He made the waters stand as a heap.
WEB.14: In the daytime he also led them with a cloud, and all night with a light of fire.
WEB.15: He split rocks in the wilderness, and gave them drink abundantly as out of the depths.
WEB.16: He brought streams also out of the rock, and caused waters to run down like rivers.
WEB.17: Yet they still went on to sin against him, to rebel against the Most High in the desert.
WEB.18: They tempted God in their heart by asking food according to their desire.
WEB.19: Yes, they spoke against God. They said, “Can God prepare a table in the wilderness?
WEB.20: Behold, he struck the rock, so that waters gushed out, and streams overflowed. Can he give bread also? Will he provide meat for his people?”
WEB.21: Therefore Yahweh heard, and was angry. A fire was kindled against Jacob, anger also went up against Israel,
WEB.22: because they didn’t believe in God, and didn’t trust in his salvation.
WEB.23: Yet he commanded the skies above, and opened the doors of heaven.
WEB.24: He rained down manna on them to eat, and gave them food from the sky.
WEB.25: Man ate the bread of angels. He sent them food to the full.
WEB.26: He caused the east wind to blow in the sky. By his power he guided the south wind.
WEB.27: He rained also meat on them as the dust; winged birds as the sand of the seas.
WEB.28: He let them fall in the middle of their camp, around their habitations.
WEB.29: So they ate, and were well filled. He gave them their own desire.
WEB.30: They didn’t turn from their cravings. Their food was yet in their mouths,
WEB.31: when the anger of God went up against them, killed some of their fattest, and struck down the young men of Israel.
WEB.32: For all this they still sinned, and didn’t believe in his wondrous works.
WEB.33: Therefore he consumed their days in vanity, and their years in terror.
WEB.34: When he killed them, then they inquired after him. They returned and sought God earnestly.
WEB.35: They remembered that God was their rock, the Most High God, their redeemer.
WEB.36: But they flattered him with their mouth, and lied to him with their tongue.
WEB.37: For their heart was not right with him, neither were they faithful in his covenant.
WEB.38: But he, being merciful, forgave iniquity, and didn’t destroy them. Yes, many times he turned his anger away, and didn’t stir up all his wrath.
WEB.39: He remembered that they were but flesh, a wind that passes away, and doesn’t come again.
WEB.40: How often they rebelled against him in the wilderness, and grieved him in the desert!
WEB.41: They turned again and tempted God, and provoked the Holy One of Israel.
WEB.42: They didn’t remember his hand, nor the day when he redeemed them from the adversary;
WEB.43: how he set his signs in Egypt, his wonders in the field of Zoan,
WEB.44: he turned their rivers into blood, and their streams, so that they could not drink.
WEB.45: He sent among them swarms of flies, which devoured them; and frogs, which destroyed them.
WEB.46: He gave also their increase to the caterpillar, and their labor to the locust.
WEB.47: He destroyed their vines with hail, their sycamore fig trees with frost.
WEB.48: He gave over their livestock also to the hail, and their flocks to hot thunderbolts.
WEB.49: He threw on them the fierceness of his anger, wrath, indignation, and trouble, and a band of angels of evil.
WEB.50: He made a path for his anger. He didn’t spare their soul from death, but gave their life over to the pestilence,
WEB.51: and struck all the firstborn in Egypt, the chief of their strength in the tents of Ham.
WEB.52: But he led out his own people like sheep, and guided them in the wilderness like a flock.
WEB.53: He led them safely, so that they weren’t afraid, but the sea overwhelmed their enemies.
WEB.54: He brought them to the border of his sanctuary, to this mountain, which his right hand had taken.
WEB.55: He also drove out the nations before them, allotted them for an inheritance by line, and made the tribes of Israel to dwell in their tents.
WEB.56: Yet they tempted and rebelled against the Most High God, and didn’t keep his testimonies;
WEB.57: but turned back, and dealt treacherously like their fathers. They were turned aside like a deceitful bow.
WEB.58: For they provoked him to anger with their high places, and moved him to jealousy with their engraved images.
WEB.59: When God heard this, he was angry, and greatly abhorred Israel;
WEB.60: So that he abandoned the tent of Shiloh, the tent which he placed among men;
WEB.61: and delivered his strength into captivity, his glory into the adversary’s hand.
WEB.62: He also gave his people over to the sword, and was angry with his inheritance.
WEB.63: Fire devoured their young men. Their virgins had no wedding song.
WEB.64: Their priests fell by the sword, and their widows couldn’t weep.
WEB.65: Then the Lord awakened as one out of sleep, like a mighty man who shouts by reason of wine.
WEB.66: He struck his adversaries backward. He put them to a perpetual reproach.
WEB.67: Moreover he rejected the tent of Joseph, and didn’t choose the tribe of Ephraim,
WEB.68: But chose the tribe of Judah, Mount Zion which he loved.
WEB.69: He built his sanctuary like the heights, like the earth which he has established forever.
WEB.70: He also chose David his servant, and took him from the sheepfolds;
WEB.71: from following the ewes that have their young, he brought him to be the shepherd of Jacob, his people, and Israel, his inheritance.
WEB.72: So he was their shepherd according to the integrity of his heart, and guided them by the skillfulness of his hands.

AMP.1:
A skillful song, or a didactic or reflective poem, of Asaph. Listen, O my people, to my teaching;
Incline your ears to the words of my mouth [and be willing to learn].
AMP.2:
I will open my mouth in a parable [to instruct using examples];
I will utter dark and puzzling sayings of old [that contain important truth]—
AMP.3:
Which we have heard and known,
And our fathers have told us.
AMP.4:
We will not hide them from their children,
But [we will] tell to the generation to come the praiseworthy deeds of the LORD,
And [tell of] His great might and power and the wonderful works that He has done.
AMP.5:
For He established a testimony (a specific precept) in Jacob
And appointed a law in Israel,
Which He commanded our fathers
That they should teach to their children [the great facts of God's transactions with Israel],
AMP.6:
That the generation to come might know them, that the children still to be born
May arise and recount them to their children,
AMP.7:
That they should place their confidence in God
And not forget the works of God,
But keep His commandments,
AMP.8:
And not be like their fathers—
A stubborn and rebellious generation,
A generation that did not prepare its heart to know and follow God,
And whose spirit was not faithful to God.
AMP.9:
The sons of Ephraim were armed as archers and carrying bows,
Yet they turned back in the day of battle.
AMP.10:
They did not keep the covenant of God
And refused to walk according to His law;
AMP.11:
And they forgot His [incredible] works
And His miraculous wonders that He had shown them.
AMP.12:
He did marvelous things in the sight of their fathers
In the land of Egypt, in the field of Zoan [where Pharaoh resided].
AMP.13:
He divided the [Red] Sea and allowed them to pass through it,
And He made the waters stand up like [water behind] a dam.
AMP.14:
In the daytime He led them with a cloud
And all the night with a light of fire.
AMP.15:
He split rocks in the wilderness
And gave them abundant [water to] drink like the ocean depths.
AMP.16:
He brought streams also from the rock [at Rephidim and Kadesh]
And caused waters to run down like rivers.
AMP.17:
Yet they still continued to sin against Him
By rebelling against the Most High in the desert.
AMP.18:
And in their hearts they put God to the test
By asking for food according to their [selfish] appetite.
AMP.19:
Then they spoke against God;
They said, "Can God prepare [food for] a table in the wilderness?
AMP.20:
"Behold, He struck the rock so that waters gushed out
And the streams overflowed;
Can He give bread also?
Or will He provide meat for His people?"
AMP.21:
Therefore, when the LORD heard, He was full of wrath;
A fire was kindled against Jacob,
And His anger mounted up against Israel,
AMP.22:
Because they did not believe in God [they did not rely on Him, they did not adhere to Him],
And they did not trust in His salvation (His power to save).
AMP.23:
Yet He commanded the clouds from above
And opened the doors of heaven;
AMP.24:
And He rained down manna upon them to eat
And gave them the grain of heaven.
AMP.25:
Man ate the bread of angels;
God sent them provision in abundance.
AMP.26:
He caused the east wind to blow in the heavens
And by His [unlimited] power He guided the south wind.
AMP.27:
He rained meat upon them like the dust,
And winged birds (quail) like the sand of the seas.
AMP.28:
And He let them fall in the midst of their camp,
Around their tents.
AMP.29:
So they ate and were well filled,
He gave them what they craved.
AMP.30:
Before they had satisfied their desire,
And while their food was in their mouths,
AMP.31:
The wrath of God rose against them
And killed some of the strongest of them,
And subdued the choice young men of Israel.
AMP.32:
In spite of all this they still sinned,
For they did not believe in His wonderful and extraordinary works.
AMP.33:
Therefore He consumed their days like a breath [in emptiness and futility]
And their years in sudden terror.
AMP.34:
When He killed [some of] them, then those remaining sought Him,
And they returned [to Him] and searched diligently for God [for a time].
AMP.35:
And they remembered that God was their rock,
And the Most High God their Redeemer.
AMP.36:
Nevertheless they flattered Him with their mouths
And lied to Him with their tongues.
AMP.37:
For their heart was not steadfast toward Him,
Nor were they faithful to His covenant.
AMP.38:
But He, the source of compassion and lovingkindness, forgave their wickedness and did not destroy them;
Many times He restrained His anger
And did not stir up all His wrath.
AMP.39:
For He [graciously] remembered that they were mere [human] flesh,
A wind that goes and does not return.
AMP.40:
How often they rebelled against Him in the wilderness
And grieved Him in the desert!
AMP.41:
Again and again they tempted God,
And distressed the Holy One of Israel.
AMP.42:
They did not remember [the miracles worked by] His [powerful] hand,
Nor the day when He redeemed them from the enemy,
AMP.43:
How He worked His miracles in Egypt
And His wonders in the field of Zoan [where Pharaoh resided],
AMP.44:
And turned their rivers into blood,
And their streams, so that they could not drink.
AMP.45:
He sent among them swarms of flies which devoured them,
And frogs which destroyed them.
AMP.46:
He also gave their crops to the grasshopper,
And the fruit of their labor to the locust.
AMP.47:
He destroyed their vines with [great] hailstones
And their sycamore trees with frost.
AMP.48:
He gave over their cattle also to the hailstones,
And their flocks and herds to thunderbolts.
AMP.49:
He sent upon them His burning anger,
His fury and indignation and distress,
A band of angels of destruction [among them].
AMP.50:
He leveled a path for His anger [to give it free run];
He did not spare their souls from death,
But turned over their lives to the plague.
AMP.51:
He killed all the firstborn in Egypt,
The first and best of their strength in the tents [of the land of the sons] of Ham.
AMP.52:
But God led His own people forward like sheep
And guided them in the wilderness like [a good shepherd with] a flock.
AMP.53:
He led them safely, so that they did not fear;
But the sea engulfed their enemies.
AMP.54:
So He brought them to His holy land,
To this mountain [Zion] which His right hand had acquired.
AMP.55:
He also drove out the nations before the sons of Israel
And allotted their land as an inheritance, measured out and partitioned;
And He had the tribes of Israel dwell in their tents [the tents of those who had been dispossessed].
AMP.56:
Yet they tempted and rebelled against the Most High God
And did not keep His testimonies (laws).
AMP.57:
They turned back and acted unfaithfully like their fathers;
They were twisted like a warped bow [that will not respond to the archer's aim].
AMP.58:
For they provoked Him to [righteous] anger with their high places [devoted to idol worship]
And moved Him to jealousy with their carved images [by denying Him the love, worship, and obedience that is rightfully and uniquely His].
AMP.59:
When God heard this, He was filled with [righteous] wrath;
And utterly rejected Israel, [greatly hating her ways],
AMP.60:
So that He abandoned the tabernacle at Shiloh,
The tent in which He had dwelled among men,
AMP.61:
And gave up His strength and power (the ark of the covenant) into captivity,
And His glory into the hand of the enemy (the Philistines).
AMP.62:
He also handed His people over to the sword,
And was infuriated with His inheritance (Israel).
AMP.63:
The fire [of war] devoured His young men,
And His [bereaved] virgins had no wedding songs.
AMP.64:
His priests [Hophni and Phinehas] fell by the sword,
And His widows could not weep.
AMP.65:
Then the Lord awakened as from sleep,
Like a [mighty] warrior who awakens from the sleep of wine [fully conscious of his power].
AMP.66:
He drove His enemies backward;
He subjected them to lasting shame and dishonor.
AMP.67:
Moreover, He rejected the tent of Joseph,
And did not choose the tribe of Ephraim [in which the tabernacle stood].
AMP.68:
But He chose the tribe of Judah [as Israel's leader],
Mount Zion, which He loved [to replace Shiloh as His capital].
AMP.69:
And He built His sanctuary [exalted] like the heights [of the heavens],
Like the earth which He has established forever.
AMP.70:
He also chose David His servant
And took him from the sheepfolds;
AMP.71:
From tending the ewes with nursing young He brought him
To shepherd Jacob His people,
And Israel His inheritance.
AMP.72:
So David shepherded them according to the integrity of his heart;
And guided them with his skillful hands.

MSG.1:
An Asaph psalm
Listen, dear friends, to God's truth,
bend your ears to what I tell you.
MSG.2:
I'm chewing on the morsel of a proverb;
I'll let you in on the sweet old truths,
MSG.3:
Stories we heard from our fathers,
counsel we learned at our mother's knee.
MSG.4:
We're not keeping this to ourselves,
we're passing it along to the next generation—
God's fame and fortune,
the marvelous things he has done.
MSG.5:
He planted a witness in Jacob,
set his Word firmly in Israel,
Then commanded our parents
to teach it to their children
MSG.6:
So the next generation would know,
and all the generations to come—
Know the truth and tell the stories
so their children
MSG.7:
can trust in God,
Never forget the works of God
but keep his commands to the letter.
MSG.8:
Heaven forbid they should be like their parents,
bullheaded and bad,
A fickle and faithless bunch
who never stayed true to God.
MSG.9:
The Ephraimites, armed to the teeth,
ran off when the battle began.
MSG.10:
They were cowards to God's Covenant,
refused to walk by his Word.
MSG.11:
They forgot what he had done—
marvels he'd done right before their eyes.
MSG.12:
He performed miracles in plain sight of their parents
in Egypt, out on the fields of Zoan.
MSG.13:
He split the Sea and they walked right through it;
he piled the waters to the right and the left.
MSG.14:
He led them by day with a cloud,
led them all the night long with a fiery torch.
MSG.15:
He split rocks in the wilderness,
gave them all they could drink from underground springs;
MSG.16:
He made creeks flow out from sheer rock,
and water pour out like a river.
MSG.17:
All they did was sin even more,
rebel in the desert against the High God.
MSG.18:
They tried to get their own way with God,
clamored for favors, for special attention.
MSG.19:
They whined like spoiled children,
"Why can't God give us a decent meal in this desert?
MSG.20:
Sure, he struck the rock and the water flowed,
creeks cascaded from the rock.
But how about some fresh-baked bread?
How about a nice cut of meat?"
MSG.21:
When God heard that, he was furious—
his anger flared against Jacob,
he lost his temper with Israel.
MSG.22:
It was clear they didn't believe God,
had no intention of trusting in his help.
MSG.23:
But God helped them anyway, commanded the clouds
and gave orders that opened the gates of heaven.
MSG.24:
He rained down showers of manna to eat,
he gave them the Bread of Heaven.
MSG.25:
They ate the bread of the mighty angels;
he sent them all the food they could eat.
MSG.26:
He let East Wind break loose from the skies,
gave a strong push to South Wind.
MSG.27:
This time it was birds that rained down—
succulent birds, an abundance of birds.
MSG.28:
He aimed them right for the center of their camp;
all round their tents there were birds.
MSG.29:
They ate and had their fill;
he handed them everything they craved on a platter.
MSG.30:
But their greed knew no bounds;
they stuffed their mouths with more and more.
MSG.31:
Finally, God was fed up, his anger erupted—
he cut down their brightest and best,
he laid low Israel's finest young men.
MSG.32:
And—can you believe it?—they kept right on sinning;
all those wonders and they still wouldn't believe!
MSG.33:
So their lives dribbled off to nothing—
nothing to show for their lives but a ghost town.
MSG.34:
When he cut them down, they came running for help;
they turned and pled for mercy.
MSG.35:
They gave witness that God was their rock,
that High God was their redeemer,
MSG.36:
But they didn't mean a word of it;
they lied through their teeth the whole time.
MSG.37:
They could not have cared less about him,
wanted nothing to do with his Covenant.
MSG.38:
And God? Compassionate!
Forgave the sin! Didn't destroy!
Over and over he reined in his anger,
restrained his considerable wrath.
MSG.39:
He knew what they were made of;
he knew there wasn't much to them,
MSG.40:
How often in the desert they had spurned him,
tried his patience in those wilderness years.
MSG.41:
Time and again they pushed him to the limit,
provoked Israel's Holy God.
MSG.42:
How quickly they forgot what he'd done,
forgot their day of rescue from the enemy,
MSG.43:
When he did miracles in Egypt,
wonders on the plain of Zoan.
MSG.44:
He turned the River and its streams to blood—
not a drop of water fit to drink.
MSG.45:
He sent flies, which ate them alive,
and frogs, which bedeviled them.
MSG.46:
He turned their harvest over to caterpillars,
everything they had worked for to the locusts.
MSG.47:
He flattened their grapevines with hail;
a killing frost ruined their orchards.
MSG.48:
He pounded their cattle with hail,
let thunderbolts loose on their herds.
MSG.49:
His anger flared,
a wild firestorm of havoc,
An advance guard of disease-carrying angels
MSG.50:
to clear the ground, preparing the way before him.
He didn't spare those people,
he let the plague rage through their lives.
MSG.51:
He killed all the Egyptian firstborns,
lusty infants, offspring of Ham's virility.
MSG.52:
Then he led his people out like sheep,
took his flock safely through the wilderness.
MSG.53:
He took good care of them; they had nothing to fear.
The Sea took care of their enemies for good.
MSG.54:
He brought them into his holy land,
this mountain he claimed for his own.
MSG.55:
He scattered everyone who got in their way;
he staked out an inheritance for them—
the tribes of Israel all had their own places.
MSG.56:
But they kept on giving him a hard time,
rebelled against God, the High God,
refused to do anything he told them.
MSG.57:
They were worse, if that's possible, than their parents:
traitors—crooked as a corkscrew.
MSG.58:
Their pagan orgies provoked God's anger,
their obscene idolatries broke his heart.
MSG.59:
When God heard their carryings-on, he was furious;
he posted a huge No over Israel.
MSG.60:
He walked off and left Shiloh empty,
abandoned the shrine where he had met with Israel.
MSG.61:
He let his pride and joy go to the dogs,
turned his back on the pride of his life.
MSG.62:
He turned them loose on fields of battle;
angry, he let them fend for themselves.
MSG.63:
Their young men went to war and never came back;
their young women waited in vain.
MSG.64:
Their priests were massacred,
and their widows never shed a tear.
MSG.65:
Suddenly the Lord was up on his feet
like someone roused from deep sleep,
shouting like a drunken warrior.
MSG.66:
He hit his enemies hard, sent them running,
yelping, not daring to look back.
MSG.67:
He disqualified Joseph as leader,
told Ephraim he didn't have what it takes,
MSG.68:
And chose the Tribe of Judah instead,
Mount Zion, which he loves so much.
MSG.69:
He built his sanctuary there, resplendent,
solid and lasting as the earth itself.
MSG.70:
Then he chose David, his servant,
handpicked him from his work in the sheep pens.
MSG.71:
One day he was caring for the ewes and their lambs,
the next day God had him shepherding Jacob,
his people Israel, his prize possession.
MSG.72:
His good heart made him a good shepherd;
he guided the people wisely and well.

NIV.1:
Psalm 78
A maskil of Asaph.
O my people, hear my teaching;
listen to the words of my mouth.
NIV.2:
I will open my mouth in parables,
I will utter hidden things, things from of old—
NIV.3:
what we have heard and known,
what our fathers have told us.
NIV.4:
We will not hide them from their children;
we will tell the next generation
the praiseworthy deeds of the Lord,
his power, and the wonders he has done.
NIV.5:
He decreed statutes for Jacob
and established the law in Israel,
which he commanded our forefathers
to teach their children,
NIV.6:
so the next generation would know them,
even the children yet to be born,
and they in turn would tell their children.
NIV.7:
Then they would put their trust in God
and would not forget his deeds
but would keep his commands.
NIV.8:
They would not be like their forefathers—
a stubborn and rebellious generation,
whose hearts were not loyal to God,
whose spirits were not faithful to him.
NIV.9:
The men of Ephraim, though armed with bows,
turned back on the day of battle;
NIV.10:
they did not keep God’s covenant
and refused to live by his law.
NIV.11:
They forgot what he had done,
the wonders he had shown them.
NIV.12:
He did miracles in the sight of their fathers
in the land of Egypt, in the region of Zoan.
NIV.13:
He divided the sea and led them through;
he made the water stand firm like a wall.
NIV.14:
He guided them with the cloud by day
and with light from the fire all night.
NIV.15:
He split the rocks in the desert
and gave them water as abundant as the seas;
NIV.16:
he brought streams out of a rocky crag
and made water flow down like rivers.
NIV.17:
But they continued to sin against him,
rebelling in the desert against the Most High.
NIV.18:
They willfully put God to the test
by demanding the food they craved.
NIV.19:
They spoke against God, saying,
“Can God spread a table in the desert?
NIV.20:
When he struck the rock, water gushed out,
and streams flowed abundantly.
But can he also give us food?
Can he supply meat for his people?”
NIV.21:
When the Lord heard them, he was very angry;
his fire broke out against Jacob,
and his wrath rose against Israel,
NIV.22:
for they did not believe in God
or trust in his deliverance.
NIV.23:
Yet he gave a command to the skies above
and opened the doors of the heavens;
NIV.24:
he rained down manna for the people to eat,
he gave them the grain of heaven.
NIV.25:
Men ate the bread of angels;
he sent them all the food they could eat.
NIV.26:
He let loose the east wind from the heavens
and led forth the south wind by his power.
NIV.27:
He rained meat down on them like dust,
flying birds like sand on the seashore.
NIV.28:
He made them come down inside their camp,
all around their tents.
NIV.29:
They ate till they had more than enough,
for he had given them what they craved.
NIV.30:
But before they turned from the food they craved,
even while it was still in their mouths,
NIV.31:
God’s anger rose against them;
he put to death the sturdiest among them,
cutting down the young men of Israel.
NIV.32:
In spite of all this, they kept on sinning;
in spite of his wonders, they did not believe.
NIV.33:
So he ended their days in futility
and their years in terror.
NIV.34:
Whenever God slew them, they would seek him;
they eagerly turned to him again.
NIV.35:
They remembered that God was their Rock,
that God Most High was their Redeemer.
NIV.36:
But then they would flatter him with their mouths,
lying to him with their tongues;
NIV.37:
their hearts were not loyal to him,
they were not faithful to his covenant.
NIV.38:
Yet he was merciful;
he forgave their iniquities
and did not destroy them.
Time after time he restrained his anger
and did not stir up his full wrath.
NIV.39:
He remembered that they were but flesh,
a passing breeze that does not return.
NIV.40:
How often they rebelled against him in the desert
and grieved him in the wasteland!
NIV.41:
Again and again they put God to the test;
they vexed the Holy One of Israel.
NIV.42:
They did not remember his power—
the day he redeemed them from the oppressor,
NIV.43:
the day he displayed his miraculous signs in Egypt,
his wonders in the region of Zoan.
NIV.44:
He turned their rivers to blood;
they could not drink from their streams.
NIV.45:
He sent swarms of flies that devoured them,
and frogs that devastated them.
NIV.46:
He gave their crops to the grasshopper,
their produce to the locust.
NIV.47:
He destroyed their vines with hail
and their sycamore-figs with sleet.
NIV.48:
He gave over their cattle to the hail,
their livestock to bolts of lightning.
NIV.49:
He unleashed against them his hot anger,
his wrath, indignation and hostility—
a band of destroying angels.
NIV.50:
He prepared a path for his anger;
he did not spare them from death
but gave them over to the plague.
NIV.51:
He struck down all the firstborn of Egypt,
the firstfruits of manhood in the tents of Ham.
NIV.52:
But he brought his people out like a flock;
he led them like sheep through the desert.
NIV.53:
He guided them safely, so they were unafraid;
but the sea engulfed their enemies.
NIV.54:
Thus he brought them to the border of his holy land,
to the hill country his right hand had taken.
NIV.55:
He drove out nations before them
and allotted their lands to them as an inheritance;
he settled the tribes of Israel in their homes.
NIV.56:
But they put God to the test
and rebelled against the Most High;
they did not keep his statutes.
NIV.57:
Like their fathers they were disloyal and faithless,
as unreliable as a faulty bow.
NIV.58:
They angered him with their high places;
they aroused his jealousy with their idols.
NIV.59:
When God heard them, he was very angry;
he rejected Israel completely.
NIV.60:
He abandoned the tabernacle of Shiloh,
the tent he had set up among men.
NIV.61:
He sent the ark of his might into captivity,
his splendor into the hands of the enemy.
NIV.62:
He gave his people over to the sword;
he was very angry with his inheritance.
NIV.63:
Fire consumed their young men,
and their maidens had no wedding songs;
NIV.64:
their priests were put to the sword,
and their widows could not weep.
NIV.65:
Then the Lord awoke as from sleep,
as a man wakes from the stupor of wine.
NIV.66:
He beat back his enemies;
he put them to everlasting shame.
NIV.67:
Then he rejected the tents of Joseph,
he did not choose the tribe of Ephraim;
NIV.68:
but he chose the tribe of Judah,
Mount Zion, which he loved.
NIV.69:
He built his sanctuary like the heights,
like the earth that he established forever.
NIV.70:
He chose David his servant
and took him from the sheep pens;
NIV.71:
from tending the sheep he brought him
to be the shepherd of his people Jacob,
of Israel his inheritance.
NIV.72:
And David shepherded them with integrity of heart;
with skillful hands he led them.

NRSVCE.1:
Give ear, O my people, to my teaching;
incline your ears to the words of my mouth.
NRSVCE.2:
I will open my mouth in a parable;
I will utter dark sayings from of old,
NRSVCE.3:
things that we have heard and known,
that our ancestors have told us.
NRSVCE.4:
We will not hide them from their children;
we will tell to the coming generation
the glorious deeds of the LORD, and his might,
and the wonders that he has done.
NRSVCE.5:
He established a decree in Jacob,
and appointed a law in Israel,
which he commanded our ancestors
to teach to their children;
NRSVCE.6:
that the next generation might know them,
the children yet unborn,
and rise up and tell them to their children,
NRSVCE.7:
so that they should set their hope in God,
and not forget the works of God,
but keep his commandments;
NRSVCE.8:
and that they should not be like their ancestors,
a stubborn and rebellious generation,
a generation whose heart was not steadfast,
whose spirit was not faithful to God.
NRSVCE.9:
The Ephraimites, armed with the bow,
turned back on the day of battle.
NRSVCE.10:
They did not keep God’s covenant,
but refused to walk according to his law.
NRSVCE.11:
They forgot what he had done,
and the miracles that he had shown them.
NRSVCE.12:
In the sight of their ancestors he worked marvels
in the land of Egypt, in the fields of Zoan.
NRSVCE.13:
He divided the sea and let them pass through it,
and made the waters stand like a heap.
NRSVCE.14:
In the daytime he led them with a cloud,
and all night long with a fiery light.
NRSVCE.15:
He split rocks open in the wilderness,
and gave them drink abundantly as from the deep.
NRSVCE.16:
He made streams come out of the rock,
and caused waters to flow down like rivers.
NRSVCE.17:
Yet they sinned still more against him,
rebelling against the Most High in the desert.
NRSVCE.18:
They tested God in their heart
by demanding the food they craved.
NRSVCE.19:
They spoke against God, saying,
“Can God spread a table in the wilderness?
NRSVCE.20:
Even though he struck the rock so that water gushed out
and torrents overflowed,
can he also give bread,
or provide meat for his people?”
NRSVCE.21:
Therefore, when the LORD heard, he was full of rage;
a fire was kindled against Jacob,
his anger mounted against Israel,
NRSVCE.22:
because they had no faith in God,
and did not trust his saving power.
NRSVCE.23:
Yet he commanded the skies above,
and opened the doors of heaven;
NRSVCE.24:
he rained down on them manna to eat,
and gave them the grain of heaven.
NRSVCE.25:
Mortals ate of the bread of angels;
he sent them food in abundance.
NRSVCE.26:
He caused the east wind to blow in the heavens,
and by his power he led out the south wind;
NRSVCE.27:
he rained flesh upon them like dust,
winged birds like the sand of the seas;
NRSVCE.28:
he let them fall within their camp,
all around their dwellings.
NRSVCE.29:
And they ate and were well filled,
for he gave them what they craved.
NRSVCE.30:
But before they had satisfied their craving,
while the food was still in their mouths,
NRSVCE.31:
the anger of God rose against them
and he killed the strongest of them,
and laid low the flower of Israel.
NRSVCE.32:
In spite of all this they still sinned;
they did not believe in his wonders.
NRSVCE.33:
So he made their days vanish like a breath,
and their years in terror.
NRSVCE.34:
When he killed them, they sought for him;
they repented and sought God earnestly.
NRSVCE.35:
They remembered that God was their rock,
the Most High God their redeemer.
NRSVCE.36:
But they flattered him with their mouths;
they lied to him with their tongues.
NRSVCE.37:
Their heart was not steadfast toward him;
they were not true to his covenant.
NRSVCE.38:
Yet he, being compassionate,
forgave their iniquity,
and did not destroy them;
often he restrained his anger,
and did not stir up all his wrath.
NRSVCE.39:
He remembered that they were but flesh,
a wind that passes and does not come again.
NRSVCE.40:
How often they rebelled against him in the wilderness
and grieved him in the desert!
NRSVCE.41:
They tested God again and again,
and provoked the Holy One of Israel.
NRSVCE.42:
They did not keep in mind his power,
or the day when he redeemed them from the foe;
NRSVCE.43:
when he displayed his signs in Egypt,
and his miracles in the fields of Zoan.
NRSVCE.44:
He turned their rivers to blood,
so that they could not drink of their streams.
NRSVCE.45:
He sent among them swarms of flies, which devoured them,
and frogs, which destroyed them.
NRSVCE.46:
He gave their crops to the caterpillar,
and the fruit of their labor to the locust.
NRSVCE.47:
He destroyed their vines with hail,
and their sycamores with frost.
NRSVCE.48:
He gave over their cattle to the hail,
and their flocks to thunderbolts.
NRSVCE.49:
He let loose on them his fierce anger,
wrath, indignation, and distress,
a company of destroying angels.
NRSVCE.50:
He made a path for his anger;
he did not spare them from death,
but gave their lives over to the plague.
NRSVCE.51:
He struck all the firstborn in Egypt,
the first issue of their strength in the tents of Ham.
NRSVCE.52:
Then he led out his people like sheep,
and guided them in the wilderness like a flock.
NRSVCE.53:
He led them in safety, so that they were not afraid;
but the sea overwhelmed their enemies.
NRSVCE.54:
And he brought them to his holy hill,
to the mountain that his right hand had won.
NRSVCE.55:
He drove out nations before them;
he apportioned them for a possession
and settled the tribes of Israel in their tents.
NRSVCE.56:
Yet they tested the Most High God,
and rebelled against him.
They did not observe his decrees,
NRSVCE.57:
but turned away and were faithless like their ancestors;
they twisted like a treacherous bow.
NRSVCE.58:
For they provoked him to anger with their high places;
they moved him to jealousy with their idols.
NRSVCE.59:
When God heard, he was full of wrath,
and he utterly rejected Israel.
NRSVCE.60:
He abandoned his dwelling at Shiloh,
the tent where he dwelt among mortals,
NRSVCE.61:
and delivered his power to captivity,
his glory to the hand of the foe.
NRSVCE.62:
He gave his people to the sword,
and vented his wrath on his heritage.
NRSVCE.63:
Fire devoured their young men,
and their girls had no marriage song.
NRSVCE.64:
Their priests fell by the sword,
and their widows made no lamentation.
NRSVCE.65:
Then the Lord awoke as from sleep,
like a warrior shouting because of wine.
NRSVCE.66:
He put his adversaries to rout;
he put them to everlasting disgrace.
NRSVCE.67:
He rejected the tent of Joseph,
he did not choose the tribe of Ephraim;
NRSVCE.68:
but he chose the tribe of Judah,
Mount Zion, which he loves.
NRSVCE.69:
He built his sanctuary like the high heavens,
like the earth, which he has founded forever.
NRSVCE.70:
He chose his servant David,
and took him from the sheepfolds;
NRSVCE.71:
from tending the nursing ewes he brought him
to be the shepherd of his people Jacob,
of Israel, his inheritance.
NRSVCE.72:
With upright heart he tended them,
and guided them with skillful hand.

NET.1: Pay attention, my people, to my instruction! Listen to the words I speak!
NET.2: I will sing a song that imparts wisdom; I will make insightful observations about the past.
NET.3: What we have heard and learned — that which our ancestors have told us —
NET.4: we will not hide from their descendants. We will tell the next generation about the LORD’s praiseworthy acts, about his strength and the amazing things he has done.
NET.5: He established a rule in Jacob; he set up a law in Israel. He commanded our ancestors to make his deeds known to their descendants,
NET.6: so that the next generation, children yet to be born, might know about them. They will grow up and tell their descendants about them.
NET.7: Then they will place their confidence in God. They will not forget the works of God, and they will obey his commands.
NET.8: Then they will not be like their ancestors, who were a stubborn and rebellious generation, a generation that was not committed and faithful to God.
NET.9: The Ephraimites were armed with bows, but they retreated in the day of battle.
NET.10: They did not keep their covenant with God, and they refused to obey his law.
NET.11: They forgot what he had done, the amazing things he had shown them.
NET.12: He did amazing things in the sight of their ancestors, in the land of Egypt, in the region of Zoan.
NET.13: He divided the sea and led them across it; he made the water stand in a heap.
NET.14: He led them with a cloud by day, and with the light of a fire all night long.
NET.15: He broke open rocks in the wilderness, and gave them enough water to fill the depths of the sea.
NET.16: He caused streams to flow from the rock, and made the water flow like rivers.
NET.17: Yet they continued to sin against him, and rebelled against the sovereign One in the desert.
NET.18: They willfully challenged God by asking for food to satisfy their appetite.
NET.19: They insulted God, saying, “Is God really able to give us food in the wilderness?
NET.20: Yes, he struck a rock and water flowed out, streams gushed forth. But can he also give us food? Will he provide meat for his people?”
NET.21: When the LORD heard this, he was furious. A fire broke out against Jacob, and his anger flared up against Israel,
NET.22: because they did not have faith in God, and did not trust his ability to deliver them.
NET.23: He gave a command to the clouds above, and opened the doors in the sky.
NET.24: He rained down manna for them to eat; he gave them the grain of heaven.
NET.25: Man ate the food of the mighty ones. He sent them more than enough to eat.
NET.26: He brought the east wind through the sky, and by his strength led forth the south wind.
NET.27: He rained down meat on them like dust, birds as numerous as the sand on the seashores.
NET.28: He caused them to fall right in the middle of their camp, all around their homes.
NET.29: They ate until they were stuffed; he gave them what they desired.
NET.30: They were not yet filled up, their food was still in their mouths,
NET.31: when the anger of God flared up against them. He killed some of the strongest of them; he brought the young men of Israel to their knees.
NET.32: Despite all this, they continued to sin, and did not trust him to do amazing things.
NET.33: So he caused them to die unsatisfied and filled with terror.
NET.34: When he struck them down, they sought his favor; they turned back and longed for God.
NET.35: They remembered that God was their protector, and that the sovereign God was their deliverer.
NET.36: But they deceived him with their words, and lied to him.
NET.37: They were not really committed to him, and they were unfaithful to his covenant.
NET.38: Yet he is compassionate. He forgives sin and does not destroy. He often holds back his anger, and does not stir up his fury.
NET.39: He remembered that they were made of flesh, and were like a wind that blows past and does not return.
NET.40: How often they rebelled against him in the wilderness, and insulted him in the desert!
NET.41: They again challenged God, and offended the Holy One of Israel.
NET.42: They did not remember what he had done, how he delivered them from the enemy,
NET.43: when he performed his awesome deeds in Egypt, and his acts of judgment in the region of Zoan.
NET.44: He turned their rivers into blood, and they could not drink from their streams.
NET.45: He sent swarms of biting insects against them, as well as frogs that overran their land.
NET.46: He gave their crops to the grasshopper, the fruit of their labor to the locust.
NET.47: He destroyed their vines with hail, and their sycamore-fig trees with driving rain.
NET.48: He rained hail down on their cattle, and hurled lightning bolts down on their livestock.
NET.49: His raging anger lashed out against them, He sent fury, rage, and trouble as messengers who bring disaster.
NET.50: He sent his anger in full force; he did not spare them from death; he handed their lives over to destruction.
NET.51: He struck down all the firstborn in Egypt, the firstfruits of their reproductive power in the tents of Ham.
NET.52: Yet he brought out his people like sheep; he led them through the wilderness like a flock.
NET.53: He guided them safely along, while the sea covered their enemies.
NET.54: He brought them to the border of his holy land, to this mountainous land which his right hand acquired.
NET.55: He drove the nations out from before them; he assigned them their tribal allotments and allowed the tribes of Israel to settle down.
NET.56: Yet they challenged and defied the sovereign God, and did not obey his commands.
NET.57: They were unfaithful and acted as treacherously as their ancestors; they were as unreliable as a malfunctioning bow.
NET.58: They made him angry with their pagan shrines, and made him jealous with their idols.
NET.59: God heard and was angry; he completely rejected Israel.
NET.60: He abandoned the sanctuary at Shiloh, the tent where he lived among men.
NET.61: He allowed the symbol of his strong presence to be captured; he gave the symbol of his splendor into the hand of the enemy.
NET.62: He delivered his people over to the sword, and was angry with his chosen nation.
NET.63: Fire consumed their young men, and their virgins remained unmarried.
NET.64: Their priests fell by the sword, but their widows did not weep.
NET.65: But then the Lord awoke from his sleep; he was like a warrior in a drunken rage.
NET.66: He drove his enemies back; he made them a permanent target for insults.
NET.67: He rejected the tent of Joseph; he did not choose the tribe of Ephraim.
NET.68: He chose the tribe of Judah, and Mount Zion, which he loves.
NET.69: He made his sanctuary as enduring as the heavens above; as secure as the earth, which he established permanently.
NET.70: He chose David, his servant, and took him from the sheepfolds.
NET.71: He took him away from following the mother sheep, and made him the shepherd of Jacob, his people, and of Israel, his chosen nation.
NET.72: David cared for them with pure motives; he led them with skill.
